text.title: Modern Python 2026 — Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules every python-modern-2026 application must obey.

rule.statement: Pin exactly one Python version per repo via .python-version. Bump it intentionally, never let CI auto-resolve.
rule.rationale: A pinned version makes uv, ruff, mypy, and CI all agree. Auto-resolution causes silent version drift and reproducibility loss.
Mismatched targets produce false positives (3.12 syntax flagged because ruff targets 3.10) and false negatives (mypy misses 3.12 features).

rule.statement: Use PEP 695 type parameter syntax (def f[T](x: T) -> T) on 3.12+. Drop TypeVar in new code.
rule.rationale: PEP 695 generics declare type parameters at the function/class without a separate TypeVar; they are the canonical 3.12+ idiom.

rule.statement: Use free-threaded build (3.13t / 3.14) only for pure-Python CPU-bound workloads. Stay on GIL build for C-extension-heavy stacks (numpy, pandas, torch).
rule.rationale: Free-threaded breaks the GIL but many C extensions still assume GIL serialisation; using free-threaded with them causes data races.

rule.statement: Use uv as the only package + environment manager. Remove pip / poetry / pipenv / pdm from the repo.
rule.rationale: Mixing managers desyncs the lockfile and breaks reproducibility; uv is fast enough to replace all of them.
